questions.question: What range of values does the opacity property accept?
questions.explanation: Opacity accepts values from 0 (fully transparent) to 1 (fully opaque). Values between create partial transparency.

questions.question: What happens to child elements when you set opacity: 0.5 on a parent?
options: Children inherit the property but can override it
questions.explanation: Opacity affects the entire element including all children. A child cannot be more opaque than its parent.

questions.question: How do you make only a background semi-transparent without affecting text?
options: Use rgba() or hsla() for the background color
questions.explanation: rgba() or hsla() alpha channels only affect the specific property, allowing a semi-transparent background with fully opaque text.

questions.question: Which value makes an element completely invisible but still present in the layout?
questions.explanation: opacity: 0 makes the element invisible but it still takes up space in the layout. display: none removes it from layout entirely.

questions.question: What accessibility concern is associated with low opacity text?
options: Screen readers cannot read transparent text
Low opacity may fail color contrast requirements
questions.explanation: Transparency reduces the effective contrast ratio, which may fail WCAG AA requirements (4.5:1 for normal text).

questions.question: What side effect does opacity below 1 have on stacking contexts?
options: It creates a new stacking context
It has no effect on stacking
questions.explanation: Any opacity value below 1 creates a new stacking context, the same behavior caused by transform, filter, and positioned elements with z-index.

questions.question: What is the effective opacity of a child with opacity: 1 inside a parent with opacity: 0.5?
questions.explanation: Opacity is cumulative. The child's effective opacity is 0.5 × 1 = 0.5. A child cannot be more opaque than its parent.

questions.question: Which is the best approach to hide an element that should NOT be interactive or focusable?
options: Both visibility: hidden and display: none work
questions.explanation: display: none removes the element from the layout and accessibility tree entirely. opacity: 0 keeps it interactive and focusable.

questions.question: When animating opacity, which CSS property can improve performance on large elements?
questions.explanation: will-change: opacity promotes the element to a compositor layer before the animation starts, improving performance.

questions.question: Which modern CSS syntax is equivalent to rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)?
questions.explanation: CSS Color Level 4 allows space-separated syntax: rgb(255 0 0 / 0.5) is equivalent to rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5).

questions.question: What is the key difference between opacity: 0 and visibility: hidden?
options: opacity: 0 keeps element interactive; visibility: hidden removes interaction
questions.explanation: opacity: 0 keeps the element interactive (clickable, focusable) while visibility: hidden removes it from the accessibility and interaction tree.

questions.question: Which property combination should you use with opacity: 0 to disable interaction on invisible elements?
questions.explanation: pointer-events: none disables mouse and touch interaction on the element, preventing clicks on invisibly positioned elements.

questions.question: What ARIA attribute should you add when using opacity to visually hide content that should not be announced?
questions.explanation: aria-hidden='true' tells screen readers to ignore the element and its content, even though it may still be visually present in the layout.

questions.question: Which media feature should opacity animations respect for accessibility?
questions.explanation: prefers-reduced-motion allows users to disable non-essential animations. Opacity-based fade effects should be reduced or removed when this is active.

questions.question: What is the default value of the opacity property?
questions.explanation: The default value of opacity is 1, meaning the element is fully opaque.

questions.question: Which approach is best for creating a modal overlay effect?
options: Both work, but rgba() is better to avoid affecting child content
questions.explanation: rgba() on the overlay background creates the dimming effect without making the overlay's child content (modal content) transparent.

questions.question: How does opacity animation rendering differ from animating top/left?
options: Both trigger the same rendering path
Opacity is GPU-accelerated; top/left triggers layout
questions.explanation: Opacity animations use GPU compositing and don't trigger layout or paint. Animating top/left triggers layout recalculations.

questions.question: What happens when you combine opacity: 0.5 with transform on the same element?
options: Both are applied; the element is composited on the GPU
questions.explanation: Opacity and transform together keep animations on the compositor thread, which is highly performant and doesn't trigger layout.

questions.question: What is a common use of opacity in disabled button styling?
options: opacity: 0.5 or 0.6 to visually dim the button
questions.explanation: Disabled buttons commonly use opacity: 0.5 or 0.6 to visually indicate they are non-interactive, combined with cursor: not-allowed.

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT create a new stacking context?
questions.explanation: position: static does not create a stacking context. Opacity below 1, transforms, and filters all create new stacking contexts.
